hyperedges.label: Sketch design decisions for request builder

attributes.description: renderer calls window.api.body.generateDto -> main relays to helper via JSON-RPC -> DtoWalker walks DTO -> returns bodyJson + cycleRefs
renderer calls db:connections:create -> main encrypts password via safeStorage -> stores JSON -> on connect: main decrypts, relays to helper -> helper opens HikariCP pool
User picks row -> helper fetches via JDBC -> normalizes types -> maps columns to DTO fields -> returns shaped JSON body
renderer builds RequestSpec -> main resolves variables, applies auth -> undici sends HTTP -> main captures response -> returns to renderer
Electron 42.3.2 + React 19.2.0 + Vite 8 + TypeScript 5.6+ + Zustand 5 + TanStack Query 5 + Zod 4 + undici 7 + Monaco + safeStorage
JavaParser 3.28.1 + ASM 9.x + Jackson 2.21.2 + HikariCP 6.x + PostgreSQL/MySQL/H2 JDBC drivers

hyperedges.label: Spring-Aware Engine (Product Moat)
hyperedges.description: The combination of SPRING scanning + BODY generation + DB body gen + CHAIN workflows + MAP field mapping that no general-purpose API client ships. This is PostmanClone's differentiation surface.

hyperedges.label: Cross-Process Communication
hyperedges.description: IPC contract: renderer↔main via ipcRenderer.invoke + ipcMain.handle (Pattern 2) with Zod validation. main↔helper via JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio (newline-delimited). Channel naming: <domain>:<verb>.

hyperedges.label: Security Surface
hyperedges.description: contextIsolation=true, nodeIntegration=false, sandbox=true, CSP in renderer HTML, DB creds via safeStorage, IPC payloads Zod-validated, no network calls except user-configured targets, read-only on Spring project.

hyperedges.label: Sketch Design System
hyperedges.description: 9 included design sketches establishing: attached badge URL bar, vertical tab strip sections, pill bar body modes, table grid variables, tree sidebar collections, JDBC URL parse connection, right tree inline rows DB picker. Dark theme with method-color accents.

hyperedges.label: Critical Pitfalls (Must Address Day 1)
hyperedges.description: The 5 pitfalls that will bite hardest: C-2 (symbol resolution), C-1 (recursive DTOs), C-3 (Lombok), C-6 (credentials), C-7 (CORS). All addressable but require building 10+ project test corpus on day one.

hyperedges.label: Table Stakes Baseline (Postman Parity)
hyperedges.description: CORE-01 through CORE-10 features that every API client must have. Ship these tightly before deepening the Spring differentiator surface. Includes HTTP methods, cURL, variables, collections, response viewer, auth, body modes, history, import/export.

hyperedges.label: Request send pipeline
hyperedges.description: renderer → IPC request:send → Zod validate → sendRequest (undici) → auth strategies applied → Agent created → fetch → response parsed → IPC result

hyperedges.label: JVM helper lifecycle management
hyperedges.description: findJava → spawn child process → JsonRpcClient over stdio → initialize handshake → status events to renderer → crash detection → exponential backoff restart → shutdown with grace period

hyperedges.label: Secret handling across layers
hyperedges.description: Three complementary secret protection mechanisms: safeStorage (encrypt DB creds with platform keychain), secretMask (mask tokens for on-disk history), redact (scrub secrets from log output)

hyperedges.label: DTO body generation pipeline
hyperedges.description: Java helper: JavaParser resolves types → PlaceholderFactory maps types to placeholders → EnumCommentEmitter appends valid values → JSON body sent back via JSON-RPC → router returns to renderer

hyperedges.label: Collection persistence layer
hyperedges.description: Collection CRUD: create/update validate with Zod CollectionSchema, write atomically (tmp+fsync+rename), read validates on load, delete does recursive rm. IPC router delegates to collections service.

hyperedges.label: cURL import/export roundtrip
hyperedges.description: Import: cURL text → parseCurl (tokenize + parse) → RequestSpec. Export: RequestSpec → generateCurl (shell-escape args). Both validated against RequestSpecSchema Zod.

hyperedges.label: Quit confirmation gate
hyperedges.description: before-quit event → check isQuitApproved() → if not, send app:quitRequest to renderer → renderer sends app:confirmQuit → approveQuit() → app.quit()

hyperedges.label: Cloud sync folder detection
hyperedges.description: When user sets data dir, path is checked against cloud provider heuristics (Dropbox/OneDrive/iCloud/GoogleDrive) on macOS/Windows/Linux. Result returned to renderer for UI warning.

hyperedges.label: cURL Roundtrip
hyperedges.description: Full roundtrip: RequestSpec → resolve variables → generateCurl → cURL string → parseCurl → RequestSpec; D-16 determinism, D-17 parsing

hyperedges.label: History Security Pipeline
hyperedges.description: Shared security pipeline: maskHeadersForStorage + maskAuthForStorage + sensitive body redaction used by both history append and collection export

hyperedges.label: Renderer-Main IPC Bridge
hyperedges.description: Full IPC surface: preload contextBridge → WindowApi type → renderer components invoke IPC channels; 14 namespaces, ~60 methods

hyperedges.label: Data Directory Synchronization
hyperedges.description: dataDir flows: user picks folder in UI → IPC → settings store → paths module _dataDir singleton → all storage modules resolve against it

hyperedges.label: Request Lifecycle
hyperedges.description: End-to-end: user edits spec → resolve variables → send IPC → main processes → response event → viewer renders → history persisted

hyperedges.label: Request editor tab architecture
hyperedges.description: SubTabs dispatches to Params/Headers/Body/Auth/Settings; all share tabId to read/write useRequest state

hyperedges.label: Database panel component ecosystem
hyperedges.description: Connection form creates connections; table tree browses; row detail displays; column mapping maps to DTO fields

hyperedges.label: Helper-dependent UI surface
hyperedges.description: Multiple UI components depend on JVM helper being online; BodyTab disables generate, StatusBar shows state, Sidebar shows status

hyperedges.label: Postman v2.1 persistence pipeline
hyperedges.description: RequestSpec converted to Postman v2.1 item format, appended to collection JSON, tab tracks source for in-place saves

hyperedges.label: Response viewer tab set
hyperedges.description: Response panel shows body (pretty/raw/preview with Monaco) and cookies (table view)

hyperedges.label: TanStack Query CRUD pattern
hyperedges.evidence: All hooks use useQuery/useMutation with window.api.* calls and query invalidation on success

hyperedges.label: Sidebar shared style exports
hyperedges.evidence: CollectionsTree exports styles consumed by all other sidebar components

hyperedges.label: RequestSpec type duplication
hyperedges.evidence: curl.ts and useRequest.ts both define RequestSpec with equivalent shape; comment in curl.ts notes it mirrors main-process

hyperedges.label: ResponseViewer tab components
hyperedges.evidence: All render response data from ResponseResult/ResponseState types

hyperedges.label: IPC bridge consumers
hyperedges.evidence: All call window.api.* methods exposed by preload bridge

hyperedges.label: Zustand State Layer
hyperedges.description: Six Zustand stores forming the renderer-side state management. Each is a standalone store (no unified root). Pattern: create<T>((set,get) => ...).

hyperedges.label: Zod Shared Schemas
hyperedges.description: Zod schemas in src/shared/schemas/ providing type-safe validation for IPC payloads, storage, and Postman v2.1 format. Shared between main and renderer processes.

hyperedges.label: Test Pyramid
hyperedges.description: Full test pyramid: unit tests (HTTP client, cURL), integration (storage CRUD, variable resolution, import/export), E2E (Playwright Electron). All passing.

hyperedges.label: Postman v2.1 Test Fixtures
hyperedges.description: Four Postman v2.1 collection fixtures exercising: minimal, all HTTP methods, variables, nested folders. Used by round-trip tests (require 20+ total).

hyperedges.label: Postman v2.1 Format Compliance
hyperedges.description: Schemas + fixtures + round-trip tests ensuring full Postman v2.1 import/export fidelity. D-36/D-37 decisions validated.

hyperedges.label: IPC Data Flow (Renderer↔Main)
hyperedges.description: Renderer stores hydrate from main via IPC, persist changes back. Tab state debounced (300ms), env active persisted immediately, history via append/list IPC.

hyperedges.label: Authentication Type Spectrum
hyperedges.description: Complete set of auth types covered by fixture files: bearer, basic, apikey, oauth2, noauth

hyperedges.label: Body Mode Coverage
hyperedges.description: POST body modes tested: urlencoded and formdata (raw body covered in other fixtures)

hyperedges.label: Event Scope Levels
hyperedges.description: Events can be item-level (prerequest/test) or collection-level

nodes.label: Multi-Process Architecture (Electron main + renderer + JVM helper)
nodes.norm_label: multi-process architecture (electron main + renderer + jvm helper)

nodes.label: Multi-DB Support Constraint (PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, H2)
nodes.norm_label: multi-db support constraint (postgresql, mysql, oracle, h2)

nodes.label: Dark Theme with HTTP Method Colors
nodes.norm_label: dark theme with http method colors

nodes.label: Core Value: Spring project as live API playground
nodes.rationale: A Spring project becomes a live, executable API playground the moment you point this app at its root folder — endpoints detected, bodies generated, chains runnable, no manual spec authoring required.
nodes.norm_label: core value: spring project as live api playground

nodes.label: Decision: Desktop App (Electron over Tauri)
nodes.rationale: Needs filesystem + DB access without remote proxy. Tauri rejected for JDBC parity gap (Oracle/H2 unsupported in Rust SQL story).

nodes.label: Decision: Spring-only in v1
nodes.rationale: Focus enables a great Spring UX; multi-stack later.

nodes.label: Decision: User picks endpoint-to-table mapping
nodes.rationale: User explicitly stated this; mappings are often non-obvious (DTO fields often don't match column names 1:1).

nodes.label: Decision: Two body generation modes (DTO / DB)
nodes.rationale: DTO mode for 'give me a valid shape'; DB mode for 'give me real-looking data'.
nodes.norm_label: decision: two body generation modes (dto / db)

nodes.label: Decision: Read-only access to Spring project
nodes.rationale: App is a consumer, not a modifier — keeps blast radius small.
nodes.norm_label: decision: read-only access to spring project

nodes.label: Decision: Chain definitions saved with collection
nodes.rationale: Chains are reusable; treating them as first-class collection artifacts (not ephemeral).
nodes.norm_label: decision: chain definitions saved with collection

nodes.label: URL Bar: Attached Badge (001-A)
nodes.rationale: Colored HTTP method badge fused to the URL input in a single compact control. Method color drives badge background. More compact and Postman-standard than separated method+URL controls.

nodes.label: Section Navigation: Vertical Tab Strip (002-C)
nodes.rationale: Tabs on the left side (130px min-width), content on the right. Active tab has left border accent. Frees vertical space for deeper content editing. Won over horizontal tabs for content depth.

nodes.label: Body Editor: Pill Bar (003-A)
nodes.rationale: Horizontal mode pills (none/raw/form-data/urlencoded/binary) at top. Content-type selector on second row for raw mode. Editor fills remaining vertical space below.

nodes.label: Variable Editor: Dense Table Grid (004-A)
nodes.rationale: Inline-editable table with columns: Variable name, Initial value, Current value, Type, Scope, Secret toggle. Won over card-list display for density and scalability.

nodes.label: Collection Management: Tree Sidebar (005-A)
nodes.rationale: Expandable tree nodes in a sidebar. Collections as top-level nodes with chevron expand/collapse. Requests as leaf items with method-color badges. Won over table+breadcrumb for matching Postman expectations.

nodes.label: Plan 01-03: Collections, Variables, Auth, History, Import/Export
nodes.norm_label: plan 01-03: collections, variables, auth, history, import/export

nodes.label: Summary 01-03: Postman Parity Data Layer Complete
nodes.norm_label: summary 01-03: postman parity data layer complete

nodes.label: Risk: Per-Driver JDBC JSON Column Quirks
nodes.norm_label: risk: per-driver jdbc json column quirks

nodes.label: Risk: Recursive DTOs Without Cycle Detection
nodes.norm_label: risk: recursive dtos without cycle detection

attributes.description: Delivers 3-process desktop architecture and fully usable Postman alternative covering CORE-01..10
attributes.rationale: First phase establishes platform, IPC contract, and all Postman parity features

attributes.description: Auto-detect Spring Boot @RestController endpoints via JavaParser scanning
attributes.rationale: First differentiator - Spring project scanning with endpoint tree sidebar

attributes.description: Two body generation modes: DTO Schema Mode and DB Data Mode
attributes.rationale: Body generation from DTO schemas and database rows shaped to endpoint DTO

attributes.description: Renderer (Chromium/React) <-> Main (Node.js/Electron) <-> JVM Helper (Java subprocess)
attributes.rationale: Core architectural pattern enabling Java parsing, JDBC, and crash isolation

attributes.description: Chromium + React UI, pure presentation, no Node access, communicates via contextBridge
Electron main process owning filesystem, storage, HTTP client, chain runner, app lifecycle, JVM supervisor
Long-lived Java subprocess owning JavaParser scanning, DTO resolution, JDBC connections
attributes.rationale: Java-specific work isolated in JVM for crash recovery and classpath access

attributes.description: Newline-framed JSON-RPC 2.0 on stdin/stdout between main process and JVM helper
attributes.rationale: Standardized error codes, debuggable, mature clients in Node and Java

attributes.description: ipcRenderer.invoke + ipcMain.handle for renderer-main communication with Zod validation
attributes.rationale: One request to one response, no correlation needed, validated at handler entry

attributes.description: OS-native credential encryption (DPAPI/Keychain/libsecret), async API preferred
attributes.rationale: DB credentials encrypted at rest, never logged, never sent off-device

attributes.description: Per-request isolated dispatcher in main process, avoids browser CORS
attributes.rationale: All HTTP through main process to sidestep CORS (PITFALLS C-7)

attributes.description: Every ipcMain.handle validates input with Zod schema; source of truth for IPC contract
Left sidebar (Collections/Environments/History), center-top request editor with tabs, center-bottom response viewer
Local > Data > Env > Collection > Global precedence with {{name}} lazy substitution at send-time
Write to .tmp -> fsync -> rename pattern for all JSON persistence
attributes.rationale: Prevents corrupted JSON on crash

attributes.description: Spawn, health check, restart policy (exponential backoff 1s->30s, max 3 in 60s), stderr->log
attributes.rationale: Long-lived helper with crash recovery; CORE features work without helper

attributes.description: Java source AST parsing with symbol-solver for type resolution
attributes.rationale: Source-level parsing for Spring annotations, DTO field walking, endpoint detection

attributes.description: Walks Java DTO classes (records, enums, Lombok @Data, standard POJOs) and produces JSON with type-indicative placeholders
attributes.rationale: BODY-01/02: Generate JSON body matching DTO schema

attributes.description: Set<FQN> + depth cap 6, emits $ref markers on revisit to prevent infinite recursion
attributes.rationale: BODY-03: Recursive types without infinite loops

attributes.description: JDBC connection pool with desktop overrides (maxSize=2, timeout=10s)
attributes.rationale: Industry standard pool; single-user desktop overrides for small footprint

attributes.description: PostgreSQL 42.7.11, MySQL 9.1.0, H2 2.3.232 bundled in helper fat-jar
attributes.rationale: DB-01: Connect to databases used by Spring projects

attributes.description: Strategy pattern: TypeNormalizer interface with Postgres/MySQL/Oracle/H2 implementations for JDBC column type normalization
attributes.rationale: Handle PostgreSQL jsonb as PGobject, MySQL JSON as String, etc.

attributes.description: JDBC ResultSet -> Jackson ObjectNode shaped to DTO schema with column-to-field mapping
attributes.rationale: DB-04: Fetch rows, produce JSON shaped to DTO

attributes.description: Case-insensitive snake_case/camelCase auto-mapping between DB columns and DTO fields
attributes.rationale: DB-05: Auto-map by name similarity as default

attributes.description: settings.json and state.json persistence in userData with Zod validation on load
File + console logging with 10MB rotation, secret-aware redaction
Build tooling handling 3 TypeScript build targets (main/preload/renderer) in one pipeline
attributes.rationale: HMR for renderer + main, first-class TypeScript, delegates packaging to electron-builder

attributes.description: Walk .java files for @RestController/@Controller + @RequestMapping annotations, resolve paths, group by controller
attributes.rationale: SPRING-01/02/03: Core differentiator feature

attributes.description: Controller-grouped endpoint tree in sidebar with click-to-prefill request creation
attributes.rationale: SPRING-04/05: Visual endpoint browsing and request prefilling

attributes.description: Generate cURL from request + import cURL from clipboard/file
Cap 100 per collection, JSON files, click loads to new tab, substring search
none/form-data/url-encoded/raw(binary)/binary with Monaco for raw, form-table for form-data/url-encoded
None/Bearer/Basic/API Key in v1; credentials in env vars, masked in UI
Ctrl+Enter (Send), Ctrl+S (Save), Ctrl+K (Quick switcher), Ctrl+T (New tab), Ctrl+Shift+C (Copy cURL), etc.
Thinnest end-to-end working slice: app launches, helper starts, Diagnose Connection probe works through full IPC chain
attributes.rationale: Proves 3-process IPC contract before any CORE feature is built

attributes.description: contextIsolation, no nodeIntegration, Zod validation, secret-aware logging, safeStorage for credentials
useRequest mutations bridge to useTabs.markDirty(), orange dot indicator, in-place save to collection items
attributes.rationale: Edit saved requests with visual feedback and in-place Ctrl+S save

attributes.description: Tab interface extended with sourceCollectionId, sourceItemIndex, sourceItemName for in-place save routing
Shared serialization from RequestSpec to Postman v2.1 item format, used by handleSave and SaveAsModal
Renderer fetch blocked by browser CORS; all HTTP through main via undici
attributes.mitigation: All HTTP in main process via undici

attributes.description: History entries must mask Authorization/Cookie/X-API-Key from first save
Bidirectional JPA relationships cause infinite recursion without cycle detection
attributes.mitigation: CycleDetector with Set<FQN> + depth cap 6 + $ref markers

nodes.label: PITFALL C-2: JavaParser Symbol Resolution Gaps

attributes.description: type.resolve() returns null for generics, inner classes, cross-module types
attributes.mitigation: CombinedTypeSolver + ASM fallback + Object placeholder for unresolved

nodes.norm_label: pitfall c-2: javaparser symbol resolution gaps

attributes.description: JavaParser sees source fields but not Lombok-generated getters/setters
attributes.mitigation: Hybrid strategy: getters first, fields fallback, ASM bytecode for Lombok

nodes.label: PITFALL C-4: PostgreSQL jsonb as PGobject

attributes.description: rs.getObject() returns PGobject instead of parsed JSON for json/jsonb columns
attributes.mitigation: PostgresTypeNormalizer checks instanceof PGobject, re-parses to JsonNode

nodes.norm_label: pitfall c-4: postgresql jsonb as pgobject

attributes.description: Password stored in plaintext JSON exposes credentials via backup/screenshot/git
attributes.mitigation: safeStorage.encryptStringAsync() before write, never log credentials

attributes.description: String->"<string>", int->"<number>", boolean->"<boolean>", UUID->"<uuid>", etc. with angle brackets
First enum value + // valid: VALUE1, VALUE2, VALUE3 comment; display-only, stripped before send
JDBC URL auto-parse grid, Test Connection with latency, Save Connection with encrypted password
attributes.rationale: DB-01/07: User can create and test DB connections

attributes.description: Right-side 340px panel with schema->table->rows hierarchy, mode switcher (by ID/WHERE/first N)
attributes.rationale: DB-03/06: Browse tables and pick rows

attributes.description: Two-column mapping table with green/yellow/red type-compatibility indicators and coverage badge
attributes.rationale: DB-05: User can override column-to-field mappings

attributes.description: PreparedStatement for byId, keyword validation for byWhere, LIMIT 100 cap
attributes.rationale: Prevent SQL injection via user-provided WHERE clauses

attributes.description: Detect Dropbox/OneDrive/iCloud/Google Drive path prefixes and show warning chip
attributes.rationale: PITFALLS m-5: Prevent data corruption from multi-machine sync

attributes.description: Modal on first launch asking where to store data, with cloud-sync warning
No anonymous usage, no error reporting, no auto-update phone-home in v1
GET=blue, POST=green, PUT=orange, PATCH=purple, DELETE=red, HEAD/OPTIONS=gray
Always-visible bar showing helper state (green/yellow/red), active env, last-save indicator
attributes.rationale: Ambient awareness of helper status without competing for attention

attributes.description: Bytecode fallback for Lombok-generated fields/getters when source parsing fails
JSON generation from DTO walk and JDBC row to JsonNode mapping
attributes.rationale: Spring ecosystem still on 2.x; avoids version conflicts

attributes.description: Java CLI scaffolding for helper entry point (--stdio, --version, --health modes)
Virtualized lists for large response trees and history lists
Caches generated bodies with metadata (source, connection, table) for reuse
36 acceptance tests covering cold start through Postman import/export; 11 passed, 25 pending
3 plans per phase: scaffold/Walking Skeleton -> HTTP+editor -> collections/vars/auth/history/import-export
attributes.rationale: Each plan ends with visible, testable artifact; sequential dependencies

nodes.label: FEATURES.md
nodes.description: Feature landscape document covering 31 active requirements across CORE/SPRING/BODY/DB/CHAIN/MAP categories, table-stakes baseline, Spring differentiators, v2 surface, and anti-features

nodes.label: PITFALLS.md
nodes.description: Domain pitfalls document covering critical (C-1 through C-9), moderate (M-1 through M-10), and minor (m-1 through m-10) failure modes across parser, security, UX, and persistence domains

nodes.label: STACK.md
nodes.description: Technology stack document specifying Electron 42, React 19, Vite 8, TypeScript, Java 21, JavaParser 3.28.1, HikariCP 6, jsonrpc4j 1.6, and all supporting libraries with version pins and rationale

nodes.label: SUMMARY.md
nodes.description: Project research summary consolidating stack decisions, 31 active requirements, architecture approach (3-process model), critical pitfalls, and phase-ordered roadmap

nodes.label: MANIFEST.md
nodes.description: Sketch manifest listing 10 design sketches (001-010) with design questions, winners, and tags covering URL bar, request sections, body editor, variables, collections, DB connection, DB picker, DB preview, chain overview, and chain link builder

nodes.label: WRAP-UP-SUMMARY.md
nodes.description: Sketch wrap-up summary recording 9 included sketches with winners: attached badge URL bar (001-A), vertical tab strip (002-C), pill bar body editor (003-A), table grid variables (004-A), tree sidebar collections (005-A), JDBC URL parse DB connection (006-B), right tree inline rows DB picker (007-D), horizontal chain steps (009-A), bottom data panel chain builder (010-B). Design direction: dark theme, method-color accents, compact spacing.

nodes.label: Sketch 001: URL Bar Composition
nodes.description: Design sketch for HTTP method selector + URL input composition. Three variants: A (attached badge, Postman-style, WINNER), B (separated controls), C (terminal-style single line). Method color conventions: GET=green, POST=orange, PUT=blue, PATCH=purple, DELETE=red.

nodes.label: Sketch 002: Request Section Grouping
nodes.description: Design sketch for organizing params, headers, auth, body, and settings. Three variants: A (horizontal tabs), B (stacked collapsible sections), C (vertical tab strip, WINNER). Vertical tab strip chosen for IDE-like navigation and more vertical content space.

nodes.label: Sketch 003: Body Editor Area
nodes.description: Design sketch for body mode selector + content-type picker + editor arrangement. Three variants: A (pill bar + full-height editor, WINNER), B (compact mode bar with line numbers), C (side mode selector). Pill bar chosen for familiar Postman-standard mode switching.

nodes.label: Sketch 004: Variable Editor
nodes.description: Design sketch for environment and collection variable editing. Three variants: A (table grid, Postman-style, WINNER), B (card list), C (JSON code editor). Table grid chosen for dense inline editing with scope badges and secret masking.

nodes.label: Sketch 005: Collection Management
nodes.description: Design sketch for collection organization and request management. Three variants: A (tree sidebar, Postman-standard, WINNER), B (table list + detail panel), C (breadcrumb + flat list). Tree sidebar chosen for familiar expandable navigation with method-colored leaf items.

nodes.label: Sketch 006: Database Connection
nodes.description: Design sketch for IntelliJ-style DB connection screen. Three variants: A (IntelliJ-style form with driver cards), B (URL string + auto-parse, WINNER), C (wizard steps). URL-parse approach chosen for power-user efficiency with Spring devs who know JDBC connection strings.

nodes.label: Sketch 007: DB Table & Row Picker
nodes.description: Design sketch for browsing database tables and picking rows. Four variants: A (tree browser, IntelliJ-style), B (dropdown cascade), C (modal browser), D (synthesis vision, WINNER). Right-side tree with inline rows chosen for one-click DB-to-body workflow.

nodes.label: Sketch 008: DB Body Preview
nodes.description: Design sketch for DB row to body preview. Three variants: A (direct overwrite + badge), B (side-by-side diff), C (toast confirm + undo). Skipped — acceptance flow already covered by sketch 007-D row click auto-fill.

nodes.label: CORE Features (Table Stakes)
nodes.description: CORE-01 through CORE-10: HTTP methods, cURL interop, variables (4 scopes), collections + folders, response viewing, authentication (none/bearer/basic/api-key), request body modes (none/form/urlencoded/raw/binary), request history, Postman v2.1 import/export, per-request settings

nodes.label: SPRING Features (Differentiators)
nodes.description: SPRING-01 through SPRING-05: Point at project root, detect @RestController/@Controller, extract endpoints (method + path + verb), resolve DTOs from classpath, sidebar grouped by controller, click endpoint = prefilled request. The product moat.

nodes.label: BODY Features (DTO Body Generation)
nodes.description: BODY-01 through BODY-03: Generate JSON matching DTO schema, placeholder values user can edit, cycle detection. Handles recursive DTOs, Lombok annotations, Jackson annotations, generic types, records, sealed types.

nodes.label: DB Features (Database Body Generation)
nodes.description: DB-01 through DB-07: JDBC connection, list tables/columns, user picks table, fetch rows + emit JSON shaped to DTO, column-to-field mapping, row selection strategies, local credential storage. Supports PostgreSQL, MySQL, Oracle, H2.

nodes.label: CHAIN Features (Request Chaining)
nodes.description: CHAIN-01 through CHAIN-05: Define ordered chain of N requests, reference earlier step's response, run full chain end-to-end, re-run single step, chain saved with collection. Manual ordered list (no DAG) in v1.

nodes.label: MAP Features (Response-to-Body Mapping)
nodes.description: MAP-01 through MAP-04: Pull field from earlier step's response into later step's body, explicit editable mappings, mappings resolve at run time, preview resolved body before running. Drag-from-response-tree UX.

nodes.label: Electron 42.3.2
nodes.description: Desktop shell chosen over Tauri for mature JVM subprocess control, universal JDBC, pre-warmed JVM helper, safeStorage built-in, CORS avoidance via main-process HTTP. Pinned to 42.3.2 (June 2026).

nodes.label: React 19 + Vite 8 + TypeScript
nodes.description: Renderer stack: React 19.2.0, Vite 8.0.0 (Rolldown-based, 10-30x faster builds), @vitejs/plugin-react v6 (Oxc-based), TypeScript 5.6+. Largest API-client component ecosystem.

nodes.label: Zustand 5.0.12
nodes.description: Renderer local state management. Tiny (~1KB), no provider, ergonomic for cross-tab state. Chosen over Redux Toolkit (overkill) and Jotai.

nodes.label: TanStack Query 5.90.3
nodes.description: Renderer server state management. Best-in-class cache invalidation, background refetch, devtools. Caches endpoints, environments, DB metadata.

nodes.label: Zod 4.0.1
nodes.description: IPC payload validation at every ipcMain.handle boundary. v4 major release with significant perf and TS compile-time improvements. Validates all cross-process payloads.

nodes.label: undici 7.x (HTTP Client)
nodes.description: Main process HTTP client. Node 24 built-in. Runs in main process to avoid browser CORS entirely. Deliberately sets Origin header to match target host.

nodes.label: JavaParser 3.28.1
nodes.description: Java source parser with symbol-solver-core for type resolution. Primary parser for Spring project scanning. Requires CombinedTypeSolver configuration. Known gaps in generic and inner-class resolution (C-2).

nodes.label: ASM 9.x (Bytecode Fallback)
nodes.description: Bytecode fallback for Lombok-generated code and classes without sources. Used when source parsing fails on Lombok-heavy DTOs. No source-level generics info.

nodes.label: HikariCP 6.x (JDBC Pool)
nodes.description: JDBC connection pool. Desktop overrides: maximumPoolSize=2, minimumIdle=1, connectionTimeout=10s, idleTimeout=5min, maxLifetime=15min, keepaliveTime=2min. Single-user = small pool.

nodes.label: jsonrpc4j 1.6 (JSON-RPC)
nodes.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio between main process and JVM helper. Java-side server accepts InputStream/OutputStream. Pin to 1.6, not 1.7-SNAPSHOT. Newline-delimited wire format.

nodes.label: Jackson 2.21.2
nodes.description: Java JSON library. Chosen over Jackson 3.x because Spring ecosystem still on 2.x. Interoperates with ObjectMapper for user's Jackson config detection.

nodes.label: Electron safeStorage
nodes.description: Secret storage using OS-native backends: macOS Keychain, Windows DPAPK, Linux libsecret. Async API recommended. Encrypts DB connection passwords at rest. keytar rejected (archived 2022).

nodes.label: Monaco Editor
nodes.description: JSON body editor via @monaco-editor/react. VS Code engine with built-in JSON language service. Used for response viewer and body editor.

nodes.label: JSONata 1.8.x
nodes.description: Chain field-path language for response-to-body mapping expressions. Purpose-built for JSON transformation. Used in renderer for {{step1.response.body.id}} style references.

nodes.label: JSON File Storage Layout
nodes.description: app.getPath('userData') with collections/<id>/, environments/, db-connections/<id>.json (encrypted password), project-cache/<hash>/, logs/. Atomic writes via tmp+fsync+rename. Cloud-synced folder detection on first run.

nodes.label: C-1: Recursive DTOs Without Cycle Detection
nodes.description: CRITICAL pitfall: Bidirectional @OneToMany/@ManyToOne cause infinite recursion in body generation. Prevention: track Set<FQN> during schema walk, emit $ref markers on cycle, cap depth at 6, respect @JsonManagedReference/@JsonBackReference.
nodes.norm_label: c-1: recursive dtos without cycle detection

nodes.label: C-2: JavaParser Symbol Resolution Failures
nodes.description: CRITICAL pitfall: type.resolve() returns null without configured symbol solver. Generic resolution incomplete. Inner-class scope confusion. Cross-module resolution needs every dependency JAR. Prevention: CombinedTypeSolver, LanguageLevel.JAVA_21, 10+ project test corpus.

nodes.label: C-3: Lombok Hides Fields From Source Parsers
nodes.description: CRITICAL pitfall: @Data/@Getter generate bytecode only, source has no getters. Prevention: hybrid field+getter walk, respect @JsonProperty, read @Builder.Default initializers, detect @Accessors(fluent=true). ASM fallback for bytecode inspection.
nodes.norm_label: c-3: lombok hides fields from source parsers

nodes.label: C-6: DB Credentials in Plaintext
nodes.description: CRITICAL pitfall: Storing DB passwords unencrypted. Prevention: Electron safeStorage for every password, never log credentials, mask in UI with reveal button, encrypted connection file in db-connections/<id>.json.

nodes.label: C-7: CORS/Preflight Failures
nodes.description: CRITICAL pitfall: Renderer fetch subject to browser CORS. Prevention: all HTTP from main process via undici, never fetch from renderer. Set Origin header deliberately. Ship 'Diagnose Connection' button.

nodes.label: C-9: Jakarta vs javax Namespace
nodes.description: CRITICAL pitfall: Spring Boot 3.x migrated from javax.* to jakarta.*. Prevention: match annotations by FQN string, never by class reference. Maintain table of known FQNs for Spring and Lombok annotations.

nodes.label: Design Direction
nodes.description: Developer-tool dense, Postman/Insomnia-informed. Dark theme (#1a1b1e bg), method-color accents, orange accent. Inter sans-serif, JetBrains Mono for code. Compact 4-6-10-14px spacing scale. Postman-familiar where it matters, IDE-style vertical navigation.

nodes.label: Feature Dependency Map
nodes.description: Build order: Foundation (CORE-01..08) → Collections (CORE-04,09,10) → Spring scanning (SPRING-01..05) → DTO body gen (BODY-01..03) → DB body gen (DB-01..07) → Chains (CHAIN-01..05) → Mapping (MAP-01..04). Each phase delivers working app.

nodes.label: Anti-Features (Do Not Build)
nodes.description: Deliberately rejected: GraphQL, gRPC, WebSocket/SSE, SOAP/WSDL, cloud sync, team workspaces, API monitoring, CI/CD, server-side code gen, auto endpoint-table inference, non-Spring backends, plugin system, AI features, full scripting engine.

nodes.label: Variable Scope Precedence
nodes.description: Industry standard: Local > Data > Environment > Collection > Global. Four scopes in v1: global, environment, collection, request. Variable types: string (default), secret (masked). Interpolation in URL, headers, body via {{var}} syntax.

nodes.label: detectCloudSync
nodes.description: Detects cloud-synced folder providers (Dropbox, OneDrive, iCloud, GoogleDrive) by path heuristics on macOS, Windows, Linux

nodes.label: Electron main entry
nodes.description: Electron app lifecycle orchestrator: ensures dirs, registers IPC router, creates window, starts JVM supervisor, handles before-quit with approval gate

nodes.label: generateCurl / parseCurl
nodes.description: Bidirectional cURL conversion: generates cURL command string from RequestSpec and parses cURL text back into RequestSpec with tokenizer

nodes.label: IPC channel Zod schemas
nodes.description: Central Zod schema registry for all IPC channels: RequestSpec, ResponseResult, HelperStatus, collections, environments, history, variables, DB connections, cURL, import/export, state save, quit confirmation, globals, body generation

nodes.label: quitState
nodes.description: Module-level boolean flag gating app quit: isQuitApproved() / approveQuit() to prevent premature exit before renderer confirms

nodes.label: safeStorage wrapper
nodes.description: Wraps Electron safeStorage async API for encrypt/decrypt credentials using platform keychain (DPAPI/Keychain/libsecret)

nodes.label: JsonRpcClientImpl
nodes.description: JSON-RPC 2.0 client over stdio: sends requests to Java helper child process via stdin, reads responses from stdout, supports request/response correlation by id, notification, event handlers, write queue

nodes.label: findJava
nodes.description: Cross-platform JDK detection: searches JAVA_HOME, PATH, where/which, platform-specific known locations (scoop, Program Files, /usr/lib/jvm, macOS JavaVirtualMachines). Requires JDK 17+.

nodes.label: electron-log configuration
nodes.description: Configures electron-log: file transport to app.log (10MB max), console transport at debug, timestamp+level+scope format

nodes.label: redact
nodes.description: Redacts secret values from log lines by pattern-matching Authorization, Cookie, X-API-Key, Proxy-Authorization headers and masking all but last 4 chars

nodes.label: secretMask
nodes.description: Masks sensitive values for on-disk storage: masks auth tokens/passwords/API keys to ***+last4 pattern, masks secret headers in arrays

nodes.label: writeFileAtomic
nodes.description: Atomic file write using tmp+fsync+rename pattern with Windows long-path (\\?\) normalization for paths >240 chars

nodes.label: collections service
nodes.description: Collection CRUD with Postman v2.1 schema: list (lazy metadata), read (Zod validated), create, update (full replace), delete (recursive rm), addRequest, addFolder. Uses atomicWrite for persistence.

nodes.label: generateCurl
nodes.description: Generates deterministic cURL command from RequestSpec with resolved URL, auth masking, and shell escaping

nodes.label: parseCurl
nodes.description: Parses cURL command string back into RequestSpec; handles -X, -H, -d, -u, -F, -G, --insecure, --max-time, -L, --max-redirs

nodes.label: tokenizeCurl
nodes.description: Shell tokenizer supporting single-quoted, double-quoted, and bare tokens with escape handling

nodes.label: DB Connections CRUD
nodes.description: CRUD for database connection configs; passwords encrypted via safeStorage, stored per-connection in data dir

nodes.label: readConnectionDecrypted
nodes.description: Reads connection and decrypts password from base64-encoded encrypted buffer

nodes.label: Environments CRUD
nodes.description: CRUD for environments with active environment tracking via settings; validates with EnvironmentSchema

nodes.label: setActiveEnvironment
nodes.description: Persists active environment ID in settings store; used by variable resolution precedence

nodes.label: appendHistoryEntry
nodes.description: Appends history entry with header masking, auth masking, body redaction, 1MB cap, and 100-entry auto-prune

nodes.label: History Security Pipeline
nodes.description: Multi-step security: maskHeadersForStorage, maskAuthForStorage, sensitive body pattern redaction, response body truncation

nodes.label: listHistory
nodes.description: Lists history entries sorted by timestamp descending; supports client-side search filtering on URL/method/status

nodes.label: importPostmanCollection
nodes.description: Parses and validates Postman v2.1 JSON; generates fresh _postman_id; adds chains extension; returns preview counts

nodes.label: exportPostmanCollection
nodes.description: Exports collection as Postman v2.1 JSON with recursive auth masking across all items and item groups

nodes.label: Path Resolution Hub
nodes.description: Central path resolver: dataDir override or Electron userData fallback; provides paths for collections, environments, history, logs, helper JAR

nodes.label: dataDir Override Pattern
nodes.description: Module-level _dataDir singleton; set once via setDataDir, all path functions use it or fall back to app.getPath('userData')

nodes.label: Settings and State Stores
nodes.description: Two electron-store instances: 'settings' for preferences/dataDir, 'state' for UI session state

nodes.label: VariableScopes
nodes.description: 5-scope variable model: local, data, env, collection, global Maps; proxy optional; D-08 precedence

nodes.label: WindowApi Preload Bridge
nodes.description: contextBridge.exposeInMainWorld('api', api) exposing app, helper, request, collections, environments, history, variables, importExport, curl, network, globals, state, body, db namespaces

nodes.label: Preload DB Namespace
nodes.description: DB operations exposed: connections CRUD, connect/disconnect, testConnection, listTables, parseJdbcUrl, fetchRows, mapRowToDto

nodes.label: WindowApi Type Definition
nodes.description: Type-only WindowApi declaration for renderer consumption; incomplete — missing body and db namespaces present in index.ts

nodes.label: App Component
nodes.description: Root component: bootstrap flow, theme application, first-run detection, tab hydration, quit confirmation with dirty tab guard

nodes.label: Bootstrap Flow
nodes.description: On mount: calls app:bootstrap, applies theme, hydrates saved tabs, sets up quit-request listener; shows FirstRunDialog if firstRun flag

nodes.label: Quit Guard
nodes.description: D-21: Listens for app:quitRequest; if dirty tabs exist shows ConfirmQuitModal; otherwise confirms quit immediately

nodes.label: Window API Type Augmentation
nodes.description: Declares global Window.api from preload WindowApi for type-safe renderer IPC access

nodes.label: Renderer Entry Point
nodes.description: React 19 StrictMode + TanStack QueryClient (retry:false, refetchOnWindowFocus:false) + App

nodes.label: Content Security Policy
nodes.description: CSP: default-src self; script-src self + cdn.jsdelivr.net; worker-src blob:; frame-ancestors none; form-action none

nodes.label: ConfirmQuitModal
nodes.description: Modal warning about unsaved dirty tabs on quit; Escape to dismiss, auto-focuses confirm button

nodes.label: DataDirPicker
nodes.description: Folder picker with cloud-sync detection warning; calls setDataDir on browse

nodes.label: Cloud Sync Detection
nodes.description: PITFALLS m-5: Detects Dropbox/OneDrive/iCloud/Google Drive in chosen path; warns about multi-machine sync corruption

nodes.label: ExportPostmanModal
nodes.description: Collection export UI: pick collection from list, browse for target path, writes file via app:writeFile

nodes.label: FirstRunDialog
nodes.description: First-run onboarding: data directory selection with cloud-sync warning; also offers Postman import shortcut

nodes.label: ImportPostmanModal
nodes.description: Import UI: file picker, preview with item/folder counts, imports via importPostman mutation hook

nodes.label: MenuBar
nodes.description: Logic-only component bridging main process menu events (menu:action) to renderer CustomEvents; maps actions like new-request, save, import-curl, toggle-sidebar

nodes.label: RequestEditor
nodes.description: Main request editing component: method picker, URL input, sub-tabs (Params/Headers/Body/Auth/Settings), send/cancel/save/copyCurl/diagnose actions

nodes.label: Request Send Flow
nodes.description: D-08/D-09: resolve variables → send via IPC → dispatch response:received → append history → invalidate history query

nodes.label: In-Place Save
nodes.description: Save flow: if tab has sourceCollectionId+sourceItemIndex, updates item in-place via collections:update; otherwise opens SaveAsModal

nodes.label: ResponseViewer
nodes.description: Response display: status row, body/headers/cookies/timing sub-tabs, save-to-file, keyboard shortcuts, event-driven response state

nodes.label: Response Event System
nodes.description: Custom event pattern: response:received, response:error, response:cancelled events bridge RequestEditor send results to ResponseViewer state

nodes.label: SettingsModal
nodes.description: Modal dialog with tabbed settings panels: general, appearance, data, network, about

nodes.label: Settings tabs (general|appearance|data|network|about)
nodes.description: Five settings categories; general and appearance deferred to v1.1

nodes.label: Sidebar
nodes.description: Left sidebar with toggleable groups: collections, environments, history, variables

nodes.label: Sidebar group toggle pattern
nodes.description: Users can independently show/hide sidebar sections via Set-based toggle

nodes.label: Sidebar helper status subscription
nodes.description: Sidebar subscribes to helper status via window.api.helper.onStatus for reactive updates

nodes.label: Splitter
nodes.description: Draggable resize divider supporting vertical and horizontal orientation via pointer capture

nodes.label: Pointer capture resize pattern
nodes.description: Uses setPointerCapture for reliable drag tracking; reports delta pixels to parent via onResize callback

nodes.label: StatusBar
nodes.description: Bottom bar showing JVM helper process state (healthy/starting/restarting/offline/crashed) with restart button

nodes.label: Helper process state machine
nodes.description: Five states: healthy, starting, restarting, offline, crashed - each with distinct icon/color/label rendering

nodes.label: TabBar
nodes.description: Horizontal tab bar with drag-to-reorder, dirty indicator, method color coding, and Ctrl+T new tab

nodes.label: Tab drag-and-drop reorder
nodes.description: Uses HTML5 drag API with dataTransfer 'text/tab-id' to reorder tabs via reorderTabs action

nodes.label: Dirty tab close confirmation
nodes.description: Unsaved tabs prompt window.confirm before closing; clean tabs close immediately

nodes.label: HTTP method color coding in tabs
nodes.description: Tab labels use CSS variable color-method-{method} for per-method visual distinction
nodes.norm_label: http method color coding in tabs

nodes.label: CycleWarningBanner
nodes.description: Displays warning when generated DTO body contains circular $ref references

nodes.label: DTO circular reference handling
nodes.description: When DTO generation detects cycles, cycleRefs are shown in a dismissible banner rather than failing

nodes.label: ColumnFieldMapping
nodes.description: Maps DB columns to DTO fields with compatibility indicators (exact/compatible/incompatible) and auto-map by name

nodes.label: Column-field compatibility levels
nodes.description: Three compatibility levels: exact (green), compatible (yellow), incompatible (red) with color-coded indicators

nodes.label: Auto-map by name heuristic
nodes.description: Automatic column-to-field mapping based on name matching between DB columns and DTO fields

nodes.label: JDBC URL driver auto-detection
nodes.description: Detects postgresql/mysql/oracle/h2 from JDBC URL prefix (jdbc:postgresql:, jdbc:mysql:, etc.)

nodes.label: JDBC URL parse with debounce
nodes.description: Debounces URL parsing at 300ms via window.api.db.parseJdbcUrl to extract driver/host/port/database

nodes.label: Test database connection
nodes.description: Calls window.api.db.testConnection to verify connectivity before saving; shows latency on success

nodes.label: DbRowDetail
nodes.description: Displays a single DB row as key-value grid with 'Use this row → body' button for DTO mapping

nodes.label: DB row to DTO body mapping
nodes.description: Calls window.api.db.mapRowToDto to transform a database row into a JSON body matching a DTO schema
nodes.norm_label: db row to dto body mapping

nodes.label: Database query modes
nodes.description: Three row-fetch modes: firstN (top N rows), byId (primary key lookup), byWhere (custom WHERE clause)

nodes.label: AuthTab
nodes.description: Authentication configuration supporting none/bearer/basic/api-key types with env var quick-insert

nodes.label: SecretField env var quick-insert
nodes.description: SecretField shows '{{}}' button to wrap values as environment variable references like {{token}}

nodes.label: BodyTab (RequestEditor)
nodes.description: Request body editor with modes: none, form-data, url-encoded, raw (Monaco editor), binary; supports DTO generation

nodes.label: DTO body generation (Ctrl+G)
nodes.description: Generates JSON body from detected DTO schema via helper; keyboard shortcut Ctrl/Cmd+G; requires helper online

nodes.label: Polymorphic DTO subtype selector
nodes.description: When DTO has multiple subtypes, shows dropdown to select which concrete type to generate body for

nodes.label: CurlMenu
nodes.description: Dropdown for cURL operations: copy as cURL, import from clipboard, import from file

nodes.label: cURL import via custom event dispatch
nodes.description: After successful curl import, dispatches 'curl:imported' CustomEvent with spec for parent to handle
nodes.norm_label: curl import via custom event dispatch

nodes.label: HeadersTab
nodes.description: Key-value header editor with enable/disable checkboxes and common headers quick-add dropdown

nodes.label: MethodPicker
nodes.description: HTTP method selector dropdown (GET/POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE/HEAD/OPTIONS) with per-method background color

nodes.label: ParamsTab
nodes.description: Query parameter editor with enable/disable checkboxes, key-value inputs, and add/remove rows

nodes.label: SaveAsModal
nodes.description: Modal for saving a request to a collection; uses specToCollectionItem for Postman v2.1 format conversion

nodes.label: Save request to collection flow
nodes.description: Converts RequestSpec to collection item via specToItem, appends to collection, marks tab clean, sets source info for in-place saves

nodes.label: Tab source tracking for in-place saves
nodes.description: After saving, tab stores sourceCollectionId + sourceItemIndex + sourceItemName for future overwrite saves
nodes.norm_label: tab source tracking for in-place saves

nodes.label: SettingsTab
nodes.description: Per-request settings: timeout (1ms-600s), follow redirects, max redirects, SSL verify; cookie jar disabled (v1.5)

nodes.label: Request settings defaults
nodes.description: Default: timeout 30s, followRedirects true, maxRedirects 10, sslVerify true, saveCookiesToJar false

nodes.label: Cookie jar deferred to v1.5
nodes.description: saveCookiesToJar checkbox exists but is disabled with tooltip 'Cookie jar ships in v1.5'

nodes.label: SubTabs
nodes.description: Request editor sub-navigation: Params, Headers, Body, Auth, Settings

nodes.label: specToCollectionItem
nodes.description: Converts internal RequestSpec to Postman v2.1 collection item format for persistence

nodes.label: ResponseBodyTab
nodes.description: Response body viewer with pretty/raw/preview modes; pretty uses Monaco for JSON, preview uses sandboxed iframe for HTML

nodes.label: Response body view modes
nodes.description: Three modes: pretty (Monaco JSON editor), raw (pre-formatted text), preview (sandboxed HTML iframe)

nodes.label: Search in response body
nodes.description: Ctrl+F search integration using Monaco's findMatches API to highlight and scroll to matches

nodes.label: ResponseCookiesTab
nodes.description: Displays response cookies in table: name, value, domain, path, expires, httpOnly, secure flags

nodes.label: Base64 response body decoding
nodes.description: Response body stored as base64 in ResponseResult; decoded via atob() for display

nodes.label: useResponse Zustand Store
nodes.description: Zustand store holding per-tab HTTP response state (status, result, error, requestId). Keyed by tabId.

nodes.label: useSettings Zustand Store
nodes.description: Zustand store for app settings: theme, dataDir, firstRun. Hydrated from bootstrap IPC result.

nodes.label: useTabs Zustand Store
nodes.description: Zustand store managing open request tabs, active tab, dirty tracking, reorder, and debounced persistence to state.json via IPC.

nodes.label: useEnvironmentsStore Zustand Store
nodes.description: Zustand store for environment list and active environment. Persists active env via IPC on change.

nodes.label: useHistoryStore Zustand Store
nodes.description: Zustand store for per-collection request history entries with search. Renderer cache of persisted history.

nodes.label: AppBootstrapResult Zod Schema
nodes.description: Zod schema for app bootstrap: firstRun, userDataPath, dataDir, theme, savedTabs, activeTabId. Shared between main and renderer.

nodes.label: Postman v2.1 Collection Zod Schema
nodes.description: Zod schemas for Postman v2.1 format: Collection, ItemGroup, Item, RequestSpec, Auth, Variable. Core shared validation.

nodes.label: DbConnection Zod Schema
nodes.description: Zod schemas for DB connections: DbConnection (with encrypted password) and DbConnectionMeta (safe for renderer).

nodes.label: Environment Zod Schema
nodes.description: Zod schema for environment variables with secret flag (D-26) and proxy URL (D-33). Postman-compatible shape.

nodes.label: HistoryEntry Zod Schema
nodes.description: Zod schema for persisted history entries: request spec, response (bodyBase64 capped at 1MB), envSnapshotId. Auth headers masked at write time.

nodes.label: verification.png
nodes.description: Screenshot showing 8 Playwright tests passing (14.4s), confirming the working application UI.

nodes.label: Test Run Status
nodes.description: .last-run.json: status=passed, failedTests=[]. All tests green.

nodes.label: HTTP Client Unit Tests
nodes.description: Vitest tests for sendRequest (undiciClient) and generateCurl/parseCurl. Covers HTTP methods, body types, query params, headers, auth, redirects, cookies, timing, 1MB body cap, structured errors, cURL round-trip.

nodes.label: Integration Tests
nodes.description: Vitest integration tests for Collections CRUD, History (with auth header masking), cURL round-trip, Variable resolution, Postman import, and end-to-end flow.

nodes.label: Postman v2.1 Round-Trip Tests
nodes.description: Vitest tests that import→export→re-import each Postman fixture and assert no data loss. Requires 20+ fixtures.

nodes.label: E2E App Launch Tests
nodes.description: Playwright Electron tests: app launch, 3-pane UI render, URL input, method picker, request editor tabs (Params/Headers/Body/Auth/Settings).

nodes.label: E2E Data Flow Tests
nodes.description: Playwright Electron tests: send request via IPC→history persist, create collection→save request→read back full spec via IPC.

nodes.label: Fixture: 01-minimal-collection.json
nodes.description: Postman v2.1 fixture: single GET request. Minimal valid collection.

nodes.label: Fixture: 02-http-methods.json
nodes.description: Postman v2.1 fixture: GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE, HEAD, OPTIONS with body variants.

nodes.label: Fixture: 03-with-variables.json
nodes.description: Postman v2.1 fixture: {{baseUrl}}/users/{{userId}} with collection-level variables.

nodes.label: Fixture: 04-nested-folders.json
nodes.description: Postman v2.1 fixture: 2-level nested folders (Folder 1 > Subfolder > Deep Request).

nodes.label: RequestSpec Schema
nodes.description: Zod schema for internal request spec: method, url, headers, queryParams, pathParams, body (none/raw/urlencoded/form-data/binary), auth (none/bearer/basic/api-key), settings (timeout/redirects/ssl/proxy).

nodes.label: Postman Auth Schema (D-25)
nodes.description: Zod discriminated union for Postman v2.1 auth: basic, bearer, apikey, oauth2, noauth. Used at collection/item level.

nodes.label: ResponseResult Interface
nodes.description: TypeScript interface for HTTP response: status, headers, bodyBase64, bodyTruncated, timing (dns/connect/tls/request/wait/response/total), cookies, startedAt/completedAt.

nodes.label: Tab Interface
nodes.description: TypeScript interface for request tab: id, method, url, body, isDirty, sourceCollectionId, sourceItemIndex, sourceItemName.

nodes.label: HistoryEntrySummary Interface
nodes.description: TypeScript interface for history entries in renderer: id, timestamp, collectionId, request summary, response summary (status, durationMs).

nodes.label: Variable Schema
nodes.description: Zod schema for key-value variables with type hint (string/number/boolean/json/secret). Shared across collections and environments.

nodes.label: Bearer Token Auth
nodes.description: Collection-level auth type 'bearer' with token value

nodes.label: Basic Auth
nodes.description: Collection-level auth type 'basic' with username/password credentials

nodes.label: API Key Auth
nodes.description: Collection-level auth type 'apikey' with key name, value, and placement (header/query)

nodes.label: OAuth2 Auth
nodes.description: Collection-level auth type 'oauth2' with accessToken and tokenType

nodes.label: No Auth
nodes.description: Collection-level auth type 'noauth' indicating public endpoint

nodes.label: Mixed Auth Per-Request
nodes.description: Per-request auth overrides within same collection: bearer, basic, and no-auth on different items

nodes.label: Pre-Request Script
nodes.description: Item-level event with listen='prerequest' and JavaScript exec array

nodes.label: Test Script
nodes.description: Item-level event with listen='test' and JavaScript exec array using pm.test()

nodes.label: Collection-Level Events
nodes.description: Events array at collection root level (prerequest + test) applied to all items

nodes.label: Custom Headers
nodes.description: Request-level headers with Accept, X-Custom-Header, User-Agent

nodes.label: URL-Encoded Body
nodes.description: POST body mode 'urlencoded' with key-value pairs and Content-Type header

nodes.label: Form Data Body
nodes.description: POST body mode 'formdata' with text fields and file upload (src path)

nodes.label: Structured Query Params
nodes.description: URL object with host, path, and query array instead of raw URL string

nodes.label: Large Collection
nodes.description: Collection with 10 sequential requests for performance/load testing

nodes.label: Multi-Level Descriptions
nodes.description: Descriptions at collection, folder, and request levels

nodes.label: Protocol Profile Behavior
nodes.description: protocolProfileBehavior object with disableBodyPruning flag

nodes.label: Typed Collection Variables
nodes.description: Collection variables with explicit type field: number, string, boolean

nodes.label: Deep Folder Nesting
nodes.description: 4-level folder nesting (L1/L2/L3/L4) with request at deepest level

nodes.label: Redaction Policy
nodes.description: All fixtures processed through redaction: Authorization, Cookie, api_key, access_token, password, token, client_secret, client_id replaced with __REDACTED__

nodes.label: Round-Trip Testing
nodes.description: import -> export -> import structural equivalence test suite using all fixtures

nodes.label: Postman v2.1 Schema
nodes.description: https://schema.getpostman.com/json/collection/v2.1.0/collection.json

links.description: sendRequest() applies API key auth (header or query) when spec.auth.type === 'api-key'
sendRequest() applies Basic auth when spec.auth.type === 'basic'
sendRequest() applies Bearer auth when spec.auth.type === 'bearer'
index.ts calls registerIpcRouter() during app.whenReady()
globals:update handler uses writeFileAtomic for safe globals.json persistence
app:setDataDir handler calls detectCloudSync() on the chosen path
collections:* handlers delegate to collectionsService (list, read, create, update, delete)
request:parseCurl and request:generateCurl delegate to parseCurl/generateCurl from curlGen.ts
Every ipcMain.handle in router.ts validates args/results with Zod schemas from channels.ts
app:bootstrap handler calls findJava() to report JDK availability to renderer
DB and body-generation handlers obtain JSON-RPC client via supervisor.getClient() to call Java helper
app:confirmQuit handler calls approveQuit() then app.quit()
request:send handler delegates to sendRequest(); request:diagnose delegates to probeRequest()
index.ts calls supervisor.init() and supervisor.shutdown() on lifecycle events
Supervisor pipes helper stderr to helper.log and logs errors via electron-log
Supervisor.init() and spawnHelper() call findJava() to locate java executable
spawnHelper() instantiates JsonRpcClientImpl with the child process stdio
handleSend calls window.api.variables.resolve → IPC → main process resolveVariables
D-16: Uses resolvedUrl from variable resolver to produce deterministic output
Variable resolution uses active environment's variables at Env scope precedence
createMainWindow() returns BrowserWindow consumed by index.ts for URL loading and event forwarding

links.evidence: cloudSync warning displayed when folder is inside sync service
onClick handler loads full request spec from collection item into request store
useCollectionsList, useCreateCollection, useCollection hooks
loadId state triggers useCollection fetch only on expand
headerStyle, plusBtnStyle, treeItemStyle etc. exported and reused
useRequest.getState().setSpec(tabId, {...}) on item click
useEnvironmentsList, useCreateEnv, useSetActiveEnv hooks
import { headerStyle, plusBtnStyle, treeItemStyle, mutedStyle, inputStyle, smallBtnStyle } from './CollectionsTree'
useHistoryList hook with search query
import { headerStyle, mutedStyle, inputStyle } from './CollectionsTree'
import { headerStyle, mutedStyle } from './CollectionsTree'
useImportPostman onSuccess invalidates ['collections'] query
window.api.collections.list/read/create/update/delete
result.headers consumed from ResponseResult
ResponseState with status/result/error pattern
window.api.helper.getStatus and onStatus
useHelperStatusSubscription subscribes on mount via useEffect
onSend triggers request send, onSave saves current spec
Every mutation calls useTabs.getState().markDirty(tabId)

attributes.purpose: Get expected DTO shape for mapping

links.evidence: HandleTypeChange switches between none/bearer/basic/api-key

links.description: PlaceholderFactory generates type placeholders (<string>, <number>); EnumCommentEmitter appends valid enum values as comments — both used in DTO body generation pipeline

links.evidence: handleTest calls window.api.db.testConnection
useEffect parses URL prefix to set dbType
useEffect with setTimeout(300) debounce calls parseJdbcUrl
Mode switcher with firstN/byId/byWhere options

links.description: cloudSync is used via router which is registered by app_entry
index.ts checks isQuitApproved() in before-quit handler to gate app exit
generateCurl and parseCurl operate on RequestSpec type from channels.ts
readCollection validates parsed JSON with CollectionSchema (Zod) from shared schemas
safeStorage encrypts DB credentials; secretMasker masks auth tokens for history/collection storage — different secret classes
redact() scrubs secrets from log output; maskForStorage/maskAuthForStorage mask secrets for on-disk storage — both target the same secret header names
createCollection and updateCollection use writeFileAtomic for safe JSON persistence

hyperedges.label: Sketch design decisions for request builder

attributes.description: renderer calls window.api.body.generateDto -> main relays to helper via JSON-RPC -> DtoWalker walks DTO -> returns bodyJson + cycleRefs
renderer calls db:connections:create -> main encrypts password via safeStorage -> stores JSON -> on connect: main decrypts, relays to helper -> helper opens HikariCP pool
User picks row -> helper fetches via JDBC -> normalizes types -> maps columns to DTO fields -> returns shaped JSON body
renderer builds RequestSpec -> main resolves variables, applies auth -> undici sends HTTP -> main captures response -> returns to renderer
Electron 42.3.2 + React 19.2.0 + Vite 8 + TypeScript 5.6+ + Zustand 5 + TanStack Query 5 + Zod 4 + undici 7 + Monaco + safeStorage
JavaParser 3.28.1 + ASM 9.x + Jackson 2.21.2 + HikariCP 6.x + PostgreSQL/MySQL/H2 JDBC drivers

hyperedges.label: Spring-Aware Engine (Product Moat)
hyperedges.description: The combination of SPRING scanning + BODY generation + DB body gen + CHAIN workflows + MAP field mapping that no general-purpose API client ships. This is PostmanClone's differentiation surface.

hyperedges.label: Cross-Process Communication
hyperedges.description: IPC contract: renderer↔main via ipcRenderer.invoke + ipcMain.handle (Pattern 2) with Zod validation. main↔helper via JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio (newline-delimited). Channel naming: <domain>:<verb>.

hyperedges.label: Security Surface
hyperedges.description: contextIsolation=true, nodeIntegration=false, sandbox=true, CSP in renderer HTML, DB creds via safeStorage, IPC payloads Zod-validated, no network calls except user-configured targets, read-only on Spring project.

hyperedges.label: Sketch Design System
hyperedges.description: 9 included design sketches establishing: attached badge URL bar, vertical tab strip sections, pill bar body modes, table grid variables, tree sidebar collections, JDBC URL parse connection, right tree inline rows DB picker. Dark theme with method-color accents.

hyperedges.label: Critical Pitfalls (Must Address Day 1)
hyperedges.description: The 5 pitfalls that will bite hardest: C-2 (symbol resolution), C-1 (recursive DTOs), C-3 (Lombok), C-6 (credentials), C-7 (CORS). All addressable but require building 10+ project test corpus on day one.

hyperedges.label: Table Stakes Baseline (Postman Parity)
hyperedges.description: CORE-01 through CORE-10 features that every API client must have. Ship these tightly before deepening the Spring differentiator surface. Includes HTTP methods, cURL, variables, collections, response viewer, auth, body modes, history, import/export.

hyperedges.label: Request send pipeline
hyperedges.description: renderer → IPC request:send → Zod validate → sendRequest (undici) → auth strategies applied → Agent created → fetch → response parsed → IPC result

hyperedges.label: JVM helper lifecycle management
hyperedges.description: findJava → spawn child process → JsonRpcClient over stdio → initialize handshake → status events to renderer → crash detection → exponential backoff restart → shutdown with grace period

hyperedges.label: Secret handling across layers
hyperedges.description: Three complementary secret protection mechanisms: safeStorage (encrypt DB creds with platform keychain), secretMask (mask tokens for on-disk history), redact (scrub secrets from log output)

hyperedges.label: DTO body generation pipeline
hyperedges.description: Java helper: JavaParser resolves types → PlaceholderFactory maps types to placeholders → EnumCommentEmitter appends valid values → JSON body sent back via JSON-RPC → router returns to renderer

hyperedges.label: Collection persistence layer
hyperedges.description: Collection CRUD: create/update validate with Zod CollectionSchema, write atomically (tmp+fsync+rename), read validates on load, delete does recursive rm. IPC router delegates to collections service.

hyperedges.label: cURL import/export roundtrip
hyperedges.description: Import: cURL text → parseCurl (tokenize + parse) → RequestSpec. Export: RequestSpec → generateCurl (shell-escape args). Both validated against RequestSpecSchema Zod.

hyperedges.label: Quit confirmation gate
hyperedges.description: before-quit event → check isQuitApproved() → if not, send app:quitRequest to renderer → renderer sends app:confirmQuit → approveQuit() → app.quit()

hyperedges.label: Cloud sync folder detection
hyperedges.description: When user sets data dir, path is checked against cloud provider heuristics (Dropbox/OneDrive/iCloud/GoogleDrive) on macOS/Windows/Linux. Result returned to renderer for UI warning.

hyperedges.label: cURL Roundtrip
hyperedges.description: Full roundtrip: RequestSpec → resolve variables → generateCurl → cURL string → parseCurl → RequestSpec; D-16 determinism, D-17 parsing

hyperedges.label: History Security Pipeline
hyperedges.description: Shared security pipeline: maskHeadersForStorage + maskAuthForStorage + sensitive body redaction used by both history append and collection export

hyperedges.label: Renderer-Main IPC Bridge
hyperedges.description: Full IPC surface: preload contextBridge → WindowApi type → renderer components invoke IPC channels; 14 namespaces, ~60 methods

hyperedges.label: Data Directory Synchronization
hyperedges.description: dataDir flows: user picks folder in UI → IPC → settings store → paths module _dataDir singleton → all storage modules resolve against it

hyperedges.label: Request Lifecycle
hyperedges.description: End-to-end: user edits spec → resolve variables → send IPC → main processes → response event → viewer renders → history persisted

hyperedges.label: Request editor tab architecture
hyperedges.description: SubTabs dispatches to Params/Headers/Body/Auth/Settings; all share tabId to read/write useRequest state

hyperedges.label: Database panel component ecosystem
hyperedges.description: Connection form creates connections; table tree browses; row detail displays; column mapping maps to DTO fields

hyperedges.label: Helper-dependent UI surface
hyperedges.description: Multiple UI components depend on JVM helper being online; BodyTab disables generate, StatusBar shows state, Sidebar shows status

hyperedges.label: Postman v2.1 persistence pipeline
hyperedges.description: RequestSpec converted to Postman v2.1 item format, appended to collection JSON, tab tracks source for in-place saves

hyperedges.label: Response viewer tab set
hyperedges.description: Response panel shows body (pretty/raw/preview with Monaco) and cookies (table view)

hyperedges.label: TanStack Query CRUD pattern
hyperedges.evidence: All hooks use useQuery/useMutation with window.api.* calls and query invalidation on success

hyperedges.label: Sidebar shared style exports
hyperedges.evidence: CollectionsTree exports styles consumed by all other sidebar components

hyperedges.label: RequestSpec type duplication
hyperedges.evidence: curl.ts and useRequest.ts both define RequestSpec with equivalent shape; comment in curl.ts notes it mirrors main-process

hyperedges.label: ResponseViewer tab components
hyperedges.evidence: All render response data from ResponseResult/ResponseState types

hyperedges.label: IPC bridge consumers
hyperedges.evidence: All call window.api.* methods exposed by preload bridge

hyperedges.label: Zustand State Layer
hyperedges.description: Six Zustand stores forming the renderer-side state management. Each is a standalone store (no unified root). Pattern: create<T>((set,get) => ...).

hyperedges.label: Zod Shared Schemas
hyperedges.description: Zod schemas in src/shared/schemas/ providing type-safe validation for IPC payloads, storage, and Postman v2.1 format. Shared between main and renderer processes.

hyperedges.label: Test Pyramid
hyperedges.description: Full test pyramid: unit tests (HTTP client, cURL), integration (storage CRUD, variable resolution, import/export), E2E (Playwright Electron). All passing.

hyperedges.label: Postman v2.1 Test Fixtures
hyperedges.description: Four Postman v2.1 collection fixtures exercising: minimal, all HTTP methods, variables, nested folders. Used by round-trip tests (require 20+ total).

hyperedges.label: Postman v2.1 Format Compliance
hyperedges.description: Schemas + fixtures + round-trip tests ensuring full Postman v2.1 import/export fidelity. D-36/D-37 decisions validated.

hyperedges.label: IPC Data Flow (Renderer↔Main)
hyperedges.description: Renderer stores hydrate from main via IPC, persist changes back. Tab state debounced (300ms), env active persisted immediately, history via append/list IPC.

hyperedges.label: Authentication Type Spectrum
hyperedges.description: Complete set of auth types covered by fixture files: bearer, basic, apikey, oauth2, noauth

hyperedges.label: Body Mode Coverage
hyperedges.description: POST body modes tested: urlencoded and formdata (raw body covered in other fixtures)

hyperedges.label: Event Scope Levels
hyperedges.description: Events can be item-level (prerequest/test) or collection-level
